content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh

## Summary

Accepted all three amendments. They each identify real policy- and correctness regressions in `handle_propose_new_bead` that can violate intended parsimonious creation behavior.

## Votes

- `fr-1-ed5cf798`: Accept
  `handle_propose_new_bead` performs an unjournaled exact-title/detail recovery pass before threshold enforcement, so sub-threshold proposals can still mutate state (dependency + Created outcome) and emit a `ProposedBeadCreated` event without policy metadata. This bypasses the configured threshold semantics and loses `threshold_count`/`existing_bead_lookup_ran` observability.
- `fr-1-919379c1`: Accept
  `parsimonious_bead_creation_policy` calls `EffectiveConfig::load_for_project(base_dir, None, ...)`, so project-level `workflow.parsimonious_bead_creation` overrides are never loaded. This silently ignores project settings like `enabled=false` or custom `proposal_threshold`, creating inconsistent behavior versus other reconciliation code that resolves project-scoped config.
- `fr-1-8eeee862`: Accept
  The early `current_count < threshold` pending return executes before semantic existing-bead ownership lookup, so strong matches are never converted to `CoveredByExistingBead` when below threshold. This violates the required policy order (map to existing first, create only afterward) and misses deduplication/commenting behavior.
